items.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.5, Advancing Towards an AI 'Super App'
items.summary: OpenAI has officially launched GPT-5.5, a significant step in its strategy to integrate AI capabilities into a comprehensive 'super app' experience.

items.title: Introducing GPT-5.5: OpenAI's Latest Flagship Model
items.summary: OpenAI formally announced the release of GPT-5.5, highlighting its enhanced capabilities and setting a new benchmark for large language models.

items.title: GPT-5.5 System Card Details Safety and Capabilities
items.summary: OpenAI published the GPT-5.5 System Card, providing in-depth information on the model's safety measures, limitations, and advanced functionalities.

items.title: Google Splits TPU into Two Chips, Signaling Agentic Era Advancements
items.summary: Google's decision to split its Tensor Processing Unit (TPU) into two chips indicates a strategic move to optimize hardware for increasingly complex agentic AI workloads.

items.title: LLaDA2.0-Uni: Unifying Multimodal Understanding and Generation with Diffusion Large Language Model
items.summary: Researchers introduce LLaDA2.0-Uni, a novel diffusion large language model designed to unify multimodal understanding and generation tasks, showing high community interest.

items.title: Google Details How TPUs Power Demanding AI Workloads
items.summary: Google's AI Blog explains the architectural innovations and operational efficiencies of its TPUs, crucial for handling the escalating demands of modern AI applications.

items.title: Bret Taylor's Sierra Acquires YC-Backed AI Startup Fragment
items.summary: Bret Taylor's new venture, Sierra, has acquired Fragment, an AI startup backed by Y Combinator, signaling significant investment and consolidation in the AI startup landscape.

items.title: Near-Future Policy Optimization for Enhanced AI Control
items.summary: A new research paper explores Near-Future Policy Optimization, a method aimed at improving the efficiency and effectiveness of AI policy learning in dynamic environments.

items.title: DR-Venus: Edge-Scale Deep Research Agents with Minimal Data
items.summary: DR-Venus presents a breakthrough in developing frontier edge-scale deep research agents that can operate effectively with only 10K open data points, enhancing accessibility for resource-constrained environments.

items.title: Reward Hacking in Large Models: Mechanisms, Misalignment, and Challenges
items.summary: A new paper delves into the critical issue of reward hacking in large models, analyzing its mechanisms, emergent misalignment, and the challenges it poses for AI safety.

items.title: ShadowPEFT: A Novel Shadow Network for Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning
items.summary: ShadowPEFT introduces a new parameter-efficient fine-tuning method utilizing a shadow network, offering a more resource-friendly approach to adapt large language models.

items.title: OpenMobile: Building Open Mobile Agents with Task and Trajectory Synthesis
items.summary: OpenMobile proposes a framework for creating open mobile agents by synthesizing tasks and trajectories, pushing the boundaries of autonomous mobile AI.

items.title: DeVI: Physics-based Dexterous Human-Object Interaction via Synthetic Video Imitation
items.summary: DeVI explores physics-based dexterous human-object interaction through synthetic video imitation, advancing robotic manipulation and human-robot collaboration.

items.title: Google Invests in First Data Center in Austria, Expanding Cloud Infrastructure
items.summary: Google announced its first data center investment in Austria, signifying a strategic expansion of its global cloud infrastructure to support growing AI and data demands in the Alps region.

items.title: Meet Noscroll, an AI Bot Designed to Automate Doomscrolling
items.summary: Noscroll is a new AI bot that promises to handle users' doomscrolling habits, curating and summarizing negative news to save time and mental energy.
